entries.title: mcp-preflight — static migration-readiness checker for the MCP 2026-07-28 spec
mcp-chaos — fault-injecting MCP proxy and agent resilience scorer
skill-collider — LLM-free static routing-collision analyzer for Agent Skills (SKILL.md)
context-xray - LLM-free static perception-gap scanner for MCP tool manifests & Agent Skills (invisible-Unicode smuggling, hidden markup, injection intent)
forkpoint - LLM-free behavioral fork-point analyzer that localizes where AI agents diverge across repeated same-task runs (OpenTelemetry gen_ai traces)

loadout - LLM-free budget-constrained progressive-disclosure planner for MCP tool manifests & Agent Skills (models eager-vs-deferred tool loading as a token-budget knapsack over static keep-eager signals; emits a deployable defer_loading plan + CI gate)

overreach - LLM-free static annotation-integrity auditor for MCP tool manifests (flags tools whose declared safety hints readOnlyHint/destructiveHint/idempotentHint/openWorldHint under-state actual behavior; emits per-tool auto-approval policy + trust score + CI gate)

contravene - LLM-free variance-aware breaking-change classifier for MCP tool contracts (classifies inputSchema/outputSchema evolution as MAJOR/MINOR/PATCH using input-contravariance vs output-covariance; computes required semver bump and fails CI when the declared version bump under-states it; also flags safety-hint downgrades and the outputSchema-vs-error-result validation collision)

trifecta - LLM-free static lethal-trifecta capability-flow analyzer for MCP toolsets (classifies each tool by direction x locus into the private/untrusted/exfil legs, detects when a toolset or cross-server bundle closes the exfiltration channel, computes the minimum-cost set of tools to gate to break it, emits provably trifecta-free per-task catalogs + risk score + CI gate)

capsule - LLM-free content-addressed lockfile + supply-chain mutability auditor for agent capabilities (content-hashes Agent Skills SKILL.md bundles & MCP server configs into capsule.lock.json, statically flags unpinned npx/pip/npm installs, curl|sh RCE, mutable git/container refs, insecure http and @latest tag pins; fails CI on drift as static rug-pull detection and on hazards above a severity threshold)

gauntlet - offline, LLM-free MCP tool-contract conformance fuzzer (the series' first DYNAMIC entry: executes each MCP tool over real stdio through schema-derived valid + adversarial inputs and grades live responses against MCP result-shape invariants - outputSchema conformance, structuredContent<->TextContent mirroring (SEP-1624), isError semantics, crash/timeout/stdout-pollution, silent-accept of schema-invalid input; emits a scored report + CI exit-code gate; pure stdlib, ships a conformant reference server that scores 100/100 and a buggy server exhibiting six ground-truth bug classes)

courier - offline, LLM-free adversarial state-custody auditor for MCP Multi-Round-Trip Requests (SEP-2322): drives an MRT stdio server as the untrusted client courier the spec warns about and grades whether it protects the requestState it hands across the client trust boundary - integrity (MUST-always-validate; blind + structure-preserving tamper), confidentiality (opaque vs client-readable/secret-PII leak), user-binding (MUST bind state to the authenticated user; cross-principal replay), inputResponses schema validation, replay/idempotency (double-execution) and unbounded state-growth; emits a scored report + CI exit-code gate; pure stdlib, ships a conformant reference server (100/A) and a buggy server exhibiting six ground-truth defects incl. a confirmed money-moving tamper exploit

blackbox - offline, LLM-free passive trace-conformance verifier for the new MCP Tasks extension lifecycle (io.modelcontextprotocol/tasks, 2026-07-28 RC): the series first PASSIVE / log-analysis entry - replays a captured JSON-RPC trace against the task state machine (terminal-state immutability, completed/failed/cancelled payload contracts, the unsolicited-handle MUST-NOT, input_required/inputRequests, tasks/update input correlation, pollIntervalMs backpressure & ttl expiry) with a 23-rule catalog, scored report + CI exit-code gate; pure stdlib, ships a conformant reference trace (100/A) and a buggy trace with eight ground-truth defects

askance - offline, LLM-free elicitation-safety & conformance auditor for MCP elicitation/create (the series first entry on the human-in-the-loop elicitation channel): audits captured elicitation exchanges against the MCP 2025-06-18 elicitation contract with a 32-rule catalog in 5 families - schema legality (flat primitive-only requestedSchema subset: nesting/arrays/refs/bad formats/inconsistent enums/unknown required), the sensitive-data MUST-NOT (flags credential/PII field names + phishing messages), perception & injection (zero-width/bidi Unicode, injection markers, homoglyph field names), usability (renderable-form defects), and response-vs-schema conformance (invalid action, accept-without-content, missing required, type/enum/bounds mismatch); scored report + CI exit-code gate, pure stdlib; ships a conformant reference capture (100/A) and a buggy capture with 24 ground-truth defects, 82 tests

understudy - offline, LLM-free safety & conformance auditor for the MCP sampling channel (sampling/createMessage): the server->client 'borrow the client's LLM' inversion and structural inverse of #014's elicitation. Audits captured sampling exchanges against the 2026-07-28 contract via a 15-rule / ~30-check catalog in 6 families - deprecation & capability (SEP-2577 sampling-deprecation advisory + SEP-2596 includeContext/tools capability gating), schema & structure (required/positive maxTokens, roles/content types, tool_result no-mixing + tool_use<->tool_result balance), context confidentiality (allServers/thisServer cross-server exfiltration), injection & perception (Unit 42 injection/covert-tool signatures, zero-width/bidi Unicode, homoglyph tool names), resource abuse (maxTokens token-bomb, expensive-model steering), and human-in-the-loop & result conformance - scored report + CI exit-code gate, pure stdlib; ships a conformant reference capture (100/A) and a malicious capture with 27 ground-truth rule defects, 46 tests
